what_it_does: This file is a generated surface-spec JSON for view A9 (area A, title 'Layers / provenance') targeting desktop. It declares the view's identity, the concepts it represents, its unrouted home journey, the UI addresses it maps to, and the components/build outputs it feeds, while leaving journeys and cross_journeys empty.
observations: It lives under _unrouted, with home_journey '_unrouted' and empty journeys/cross_journeys.
build_to references components (canvas, toolbar), a design-lint command, and a tokens-to-CSS pipeline.
Status is 'planned' and platform is 'desktop' only.
It includes a provenance note that it was built by a Claude Code session and warns never to invent an address.
standouts: Explicit unrouted state with empty journey wiring.
Dual address mapping to both canvas and toolbar layer UI.
build_to mixes components, a lint gate, and a single token-derived look-source.
Represents two layer-related concepts at once.
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses the directory path as a journey spine tree.
References UI targets with a ui:// address scheme.
Breaks typical routing convention by leaving journeys/cross_journeys empty, which is consistent with its _unrouted status.
concerns: Empty journeys/cross_journeys may leave the surface orphaned until routing is resolved.
The two represented concepts ('CAN-layers', 'NODE-layer') are not differentiated in this file.
Status is 'planned' while build_to already specifies implementation targets.
The design_lint value contains parenthetical placeholder phrasing ('the FORM gate').
notes: A9 is an unrouted desktop surface spec for a Layers/Provenance view that maps to canvas and toolbar layer addresses but has no journey wiring yet.
questions: What condition moves this surface out of _unrouted?
How do 'CAN-layers' and 'NODE-layer' differ or relate?
Is the design_lint command string exact or a template?
Why does one view target both canvas and toolbar addresses?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a generated surface-spec for view A9; the content matches that purpose, though it is explicitly unrouted with empty journey references.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a desktop view that exposes layer/provenance information across both the canvas and toolbar, while recording how it was generated and constraining its look-source to a single token-derived CSS file.
novelty: The explicit _unrouted state with empty journey arrays and a self-documenting provenance note that forbids invented addresses is distinctive.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, self-described surface definition for a layers/provenance view, including its address targets, build artifacts, and an explicit unrouted status that future fusion queries can use to detect missing journey wiring.
summary_for_embedding: A9.json is a planned desktop surface specification for a Layers/Provenance view, mapping to canvas and toolbar layer addresses with empty journey wiring and build-to references for components, lint, and tokens.
intention_for_embedding: This file was generated to record the intended surface layout, address targets, and build outputs for a layers/provenance UI before it is wired into a journey spine.
